 {"id":"bd-2vh3.1","title":"Tier 1: Ephemeral repo routing","description":"Add routing.ephemeral config option to route ephemeral=true issues to separate location.

## Changes Required

1. Add `routing.ephemeral` config option (default: empty = disabled)
2. Update routing logic in `determineRepo()` to check ephemeral flag
3. Update `bd create` to respect ephemeral routing
4. Update import/export for multi-location support
5. Ephemeral repo can be:
   - Separate git repo (~/.beads-ephemeral)
   - Non-git directory (just filesystem)
   - Same repo, different branch (future)

## Config

```bash
bd config set routing.ephemeral "~/.beads-ephemeral"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bd create "test" --ephemeral` creates in ephemeral repo when configured
- `bd list` shows issues from both repos
- Ephemeral repo never synced to remote","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:26.648052-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:01.815357-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:01.815357-08:00","deleted_by":"stevey","delete_reason":"manual delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.2","title":"Tier 1: Ephemeral repo routing","description":"Simplified: Make mol spawn set ephemeral=true on spawned issues.

## The Fix

Modify cloneSubgraph() in template.go to set Ephemeral: true:

```go
// template.go:474
newIssue := &types.Issue{
    Title:      substituteVariables(oldIssue.Title, vars),
    // ... existing fields ...
    Ephemeral:  true,  // ADD THIS LINE
}
```

## Optional: Add --persistent flag

Add flag to bd mol spawn for when you want spawned issues to persist:

```bash
bd mol spawn mol-code-review --var pr=123  # ephemeral (default)
bd mol spawn mol-code-review --var pr=123 --persistent  # not ephemeral
```

## Why This Is Simpler Than Original Design

Original design proposed separate ephemeral repo routing. After code review:

- Ephemeral field already exists in schema
- bd cleanup --ephemeral already works
- No new config needed
- No multi-repo complexity

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol spawn creates issues with ephemeral=true
- bd cleanup --ephemeral -f deletes them after closing
- --persistent flag opts out of ephemeral
- Existing molecules continue to work","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:36.661604-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:43:22.990244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:43:22.990244-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: mol spawn now creates ephemeral issues by default. Use --persistent to opt out. Changes in template.go, mol.go, mol_spawn.go, mol_bond.go, mol_run.go.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:36.662118-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.3","title":"Tier 2: Basic bd mol squash command","description":"Add bd mol squash command for basic molecule execution compression.

## Command

bd mol squash <molecule-id> [flags]
  --dry-run         Preview what would be squashed
  --keep-children   Don't delete ephemeral children after squash
  --json            JSON output

## Implementation

1. Find all ephemeral children of molecule (parent-child deps)
2. Concatenate child descriptions/notes into digest
3. Create digest issue in main repo with:
   - Title: 'Molecule Execution Summary: <original-title>'
   - digest_of: [list of squashed child IDs]
   - ephemeral: false (digest is permanent)
4. Delete ephemeral children (unless --keep-children)
5. Link digest to parent work item

## Schema Changes

Add to Issue struct:
- SquashedAt *time.Time
- SquashDigest string (ID of digest)
- DigestOf []string (IDs of squashed children)

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol squash <id> creates digest, removes children
- --dry-run shows preview
- Digest has proper metadata linking","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338114-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:53:58.974433-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:53:58.974433-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented bd mol squash command with tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338636-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.601321-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vh3.4","title":"Tier 3: AI-powered squash summarization","description":"Use Haiku for intelligent molecule execution summarization.

## Enhancement to bd mol squash

Instead of simple concatenation, use Claude Haiku to generate intelligent summary:

## Summary Format

- Execution metrics (duration, steps completed)
- Key decisions made during execution
- Discoveries (new issues filed)
- Changes (files modified, tests added)
- Validation status

## Config

bd config set squash.model 'claude-3-5-haiku-20241022'
bd config set squash.max_tokens 2000

## Implementation

1. Collect all child issue content
2. Build prompt for Haiku summarization
3. Generate structured digest
4. Handle API errors gracefully (fallback to concatenation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Digest is coherent summary, not just concatenation
- Key decisions extracted from verbose logs
- Metrics computed (duration, changes, etc.)
- Works without API key (fallback mode)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.733264-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.698686-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2vh3.4","title":"Tier 3: AI-powered squash summarization","description":"## Design: Agent-Provided Summarization (Inversion of Control)

bd is a tool FOR agents, not an agent itself. The calling agent provides
the summary; bd just stores it.

### API

```bash
# Agent generates summary, passes to bd
bd mol squash bd-xxx --summary "Agent-generated summary here"

# Without --summary, falls back to basic concatenation
bd mol squash bd-xxx
```

### Gas Town Integration Pattern

```go
// In polecat completion handler or witness
raw := exec.Command("bd", "mol", "show", molID, "--json").Output()
summary := callHaiku(buildSummaryPrompt(raw))  // agent's job
exec.Command("bd", "mol", "squash", molID, "--summary", summary).Run()
```

### Why This Design

| Concern | bd's job | Agent's job |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| Store data | ✅ | |
| Query data | ✅ | |
| Generate summaries | | ✅ |
| Call LLMs | | ✅ |
| Manage API keys | | ✅ |

### Implementation Status

- [x] --summary flag added to bd mol squash
- [x] Tests for agent-provided summary
- [ ] Gas Town integration (separate task)

### Acceptance Criteria

- ✅ bd mol squash --summary uses provided text
- ✅ Without --summary, falls back to concatenation
- ✅ No LLM calls in bd itself","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --summary flag for agent-provided summaries. bd stays a pure tool; agents provide the intelligence.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.733264-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.698686-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.5","title":"Tier 4: Auto-squash on molecule completion","description":"Automatically squash molecules when they reach terminal state.

## Integration Points

1. Hook into molecule completion handler
2. Detect when all steps are done/failed
3. Trigger squash automatically

## Config

bd config set mol.auto_squash true  # Default: false
bd config set mol.auto_squash_on_success true  # Only on success
bd config set mol.auto_squash_delay '5m'  # Wait before squash

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Post-Completion Hook
In mol completion handler:
- Check if auto_squash enabled
- Call Squash() after terminal state

### Option B: Git Hook
In .beads/hooks/post-commit:
- bd mol squash --auto

### Option C: Daemon Background Task
- Daemon periodically checks for squashable molecules
- Squashes in background

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completed molecules auto-squash without manual intervention
- Configurable delay before squash
- Option to squash only on success vs always
- Works with both daemon and no-daemon modes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.346152-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.797141-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-5qim","title":"Optimize GetReadyWork performance - 752ms on 10K database (target: <50ms)","notes":"# Performance Analysis (10K Issue Database)

Analyzed using CPU profiles from benchmark suite on Apple M2 Pro.

## Operation Performance

| Operation                        | Time    | Allocations | Memory |
|----------------------------------|---------|-------------|--------|
| bd ready (GetReadyWork)          | ~752ms  | 167,466     | 16MB   |
| bd list (SearchIssues no filter) | ~11.6ms | 89,214      | 5.8MB  |
| bd list (SearchIssues filtered)  | ~9.2ms  | 62,365      | 3.5MB  |
| bd create (CreateIssue)          | ~2.6ms  | 146         | 8.6KB  |
| bd update (UpdateIssue)          | ~0.32ms | 364         | 15KB   |
| bd close (UpdateIssue)           | ~0.32ms | 364         | 15KB   |

**Target: <50ms for all operations on 10K database**

**Current issue: GetReadyWork is 15x over target (752ms vs 50ms)**

## Root Cause

GetReadyWork (internal/storage/sqlite/ready.go:90-128) uses recursive CTE to propagate blocking:
- 65x slower than SearchIssues
- Recalculates entire blocked issue tree on every call
- Algorithm:
  1. Find directly blocked issues via 'blocks' dependencies
  2. Recursively propagate blockage to descendants (max depth: 50)
  3. Exclude all blocked issues from results

## CPU Profile Analysis

- Database syscalls (pthread_cond_signal, syscall6): ~75%
- SQLite engine overhead: inherent to recursive CTE
- Application code (query construction): <1%

**Bottleneck is the recursive CTE query execution, not application code.**

## Optimization Recommendations

### High Impact (Likely to achieve <50ms target)

1. **Cache blocked issue calculation**
   - Add `blocked_issues` table updated on dependency changes
   - Trade write complexity for read speed (ready called >> dependency changes)
   - Eliminates recursive CTE on every read

2. **Add/verify database indexes**
   ```sql
   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_blocked 
     ON dependencies(issue_id, type, depends_on_id);
   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status 
     ON issues(status);
   ```

### Medium Impact

3. **Reduce allocations** (167K allocations for GetReadyWork)
   - Profile `scanIssues()` for object pooling opportunities
   - Reuse slice capacity for repeated calls

### Low Impact (Not recommended)
- Query optimization for CRUD operations (already <3ms)
- Connection pooling tuning (not showing in profiles)

## Verification

Run benchmarks to validate optimization:
```bash
make bench-quick
go tool pprof -http=:8080 internal/storage/sqlite/bench-cpu-*.prof
```

Profile files automatically generated in `internal/storage/sqlite/`.","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-14T09:02:46.507526-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.534258-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:21:37.918868-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-66l4","title":"Runtime bonding: bd mol attach","description":"Attach a molecule to an already-running workflow.

COMMAND: bd mol attach <epic-id> <proto> [--after <issue-id>]

BEHAVIOR:
- Resolve running epic and proto
- Spawn proto as new subtree
- Wire to specified attachment point (or epic root)
- Handle in-progress issues: new work doesn't block completed work

USE CASES:
- Discovered need for docs while implementing feature
- Hotfix needs attaching to release workflow
- Additional testing scope identified mid-flight

FLAGS:
- --after ISSUE: Specific attachment point within epic
- --type: sequential (default) or parallel
- --var: Variables for the attached proto

CONSIDERATIONS:
- What if epic is already closed? Error or reopen?
- What if attachment point issue is closed? Attach as ready-to-work?","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:16.920483-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","close_reason":"Merged into bd-o91r: bond command handles all bonding cases polymorphically","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.435542-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o91r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.813782-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

This bypasses tombstone creation, deletion tracking (deletions.jsonl), and dependency cleanup. Could cause issues with deletion propagation across clones.

 {"id":"bd-68e4","title":"doctor --fix should export when DB has more issues than JSONL","description":"When 'bd doctor' detects a count mismatch (DB has more issues than JSONL), it currently recommends 'bd sync --import-only', which imports JSONL into DB. But JSONL is the source of truth, not the DB.

**Current behavior:**
- Doctor detects: DB has 355 issues, JSONL has 292
- Recommends: 'bd sync --import-only' 
- User runs it: Returns '0 created, 0 updated' (no-op, because JSONL hasn't changed)
- User is stuck

**Root cause:**
The doctor fix is one-directional (JSONL→DB) when it should be bidirectional. If DB has MORE issues, they haven't been exported yet - the fix should be 'bd export' (DB→JSONL), not import.

**Desired fix:**
In fix.DBJSONLSync(), detect which has more data:
- If DB > JSONL: Run 'bd export' to sync JSONL (since DB is the working copy)
- If JSONL > DB: Run 'bd sync --import-only' to import (JSONL is source of truth)
- If equal but timestamps differ: Detect based on file mtime

This makes 'bd doctor --fix' actually fix the problem instead of being a no-op.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:17:20.994319182-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00"}

+{"id":"bd-8wgo","title":"bd merge omits priority:0 due to omitempty JSON tag","description":"GitHub issue #671. The merge code in internal/merge/merge.go uses 'omitempty' on the Priority field, which causes priority:0 (P0/critical) to be dropped from JSON output since 0 is Go's zero value for int. Fix: either remove omitempty from Priority field or use a pointer (*int). This affects the git merge driver and causes P0 issues to lose their priority.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:35:15.083146-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:35:15.083146-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-95k8","title":"Pinned field available in beads v0.37.0","description":"Hey max,

Heads up on your mail overhaul work:

1. **Pinned field is available** - beads v0.37.0 (released by dave earlier) includes the pinned field on issues. You'll want to add this to BeadsMessage in types.go.

2. **Database migration** - Check if existing .beads databases need migration to support the pinned field. Run `bd doctor` to see if it flags anything.

3. **Sorting task** - Once you have the pinned field, gt-ngu1 (pinned beads first in mail inbox) needs implementing. Since messages now come from `bd list --type=message`, you'll need to either:
   - Sort in listBeads() after fetching, or
   - Ensure bd list returns pinned items first (may already do this?)

Check what version of bd you're building against.

-- Mayor","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"gastown/crew/max","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:51:57.315956-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:51:57.315956-08:00","labels":["from:beads-crew-dave","thread:thread-71ac20c7e432"]}

 {"id":"bd-lw0x","title":"Fix bd sync race condition with daemon causing dirty working directory","description":"After bd sync completes with sync.branch mode, subsequent bd commands or daemon file watcher would see a hash mismatch and trigger auto-import, which then schedules re-export, dirtying the working directory.

**Root cause:**
1. bd sync exports JSONL with NEW content (hash H1)
2. bd sync updates jsonl_content_hash = H1 in DB
3. bd sync restores JSONL from HEAD (OLD content, hash H0)
4. Now: file hash = H0, DB hash = H1 (MISMATCH)
5. Daemon or next CLI command sees mismatch, imports from OLD JSONL
6. Import triggers re-export → file is dirty

**Fix:**
After restoreBeadsDirFromBranch(), update jsonl_content_hash to match the restored file's hash. This ensures daemon and CLI see file hash = DB hash → no spurious import/export cycle.

Related: bd-c83r (multiple daemon prevention)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T06:42:17.130839-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T06:43:33.329042-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T06:43:33.329042-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-lxzx","title":"Add close_reason to JSONL export format documentation","description":"PR #551 now persists close_reason to the database, but there's a question about whether this field should be exported to JSONL format.

## Current State
- close_reason is stored in issues.close_reason column
- close_reason is also stored in events table (audit trail)
- The JSONL export format may or may not include close_reason

## Questions
1. Should close_reason be exported to JSONL format?
2. If yes, where should it go (root level or nested in events)?
3. Should there be any special handling to avoid duplication?
4. How should close_reason be handled during JSONL import?

## Why This Matters
- JSONL is the git-friendly sync format
- Other beads instances import from JSONL
- close_reason is meaningful data that should be preserved across clones

## Suggested Action
- Check if close_reason is currently exported in JSONL
- If not, add it to the export schema
- Document the field in JSONL format spec
- Add tests for round-trip (export -> import -> verify close_reason)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.414916-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.414916-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxzx","depends_on_id":"bd-z86n","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.416131-08:00","created_by":"stevey","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mql4","title":"getLocalSyncBranch silently ignores YAML parse errors","description":"In autoimport.go:170-172, YAML parsing errors are silently ignored. If a user has malformed YAML in config.yaml, sync-branch will just silently be empty with no feedback.

Recommendation: Add debug logging since this function is only called during auto-import, and debugging silent failures is painful.

Add: debug.Logf("Warning: failed to parse config.yaml: %v", err)","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T02:03:44.217728-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T02:03:44.217728-08:00"}
